On 17 December 2008, after a Spanish-Moroccan summit, the Moroccan Prime Minister Abbas El Fassi declared that his government was "studying" the possibility to grant voting rights for municipal elections to foreign residents in Morocco.

tavily.raw: {"query":"July 27 2026 municipal elections Venezuela turnout results opposition boycott","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://venezuelanalysis.com/news/venezuela-socialist-party-begins-candidate-selection-ahead-of-municipal-vote-as-opposition-mayors-seek-reelection","title":"Venezuela: Socialist Party Begins Candidate Selection for Municipal Vote as Opposition Mayors Seek Reelection - Venezuelanalysis","content":"# Venezuela: Socialist Party Begins Candidate Selection for Municipal Vote as Opposition Mayors Seek Reelection. Venezuelan voters will also take part in a popular consultation to choose local state-funded projects. Venezuelans will choose 335 mayors and 2,471 municipal councillors on July 27. Caracas, June 9, 2025 (venezuelanalysis.com) – Venezuela’s United Socialist Party (PSUV) and political allies held thousands of assemblies to propose candidates for upcoming municipal elections on July 27. However, turnout for the upcoming vote may suffer as far-right forces, led by María Corina Machado, continue to urge a boycott of elections as they look to enforce their claim of victory in the July 2024 presidential contest. The July 27 municipal vote coincides with a National Popular Consultation, an electoral process that sees communities choose local projects to receive state funding and be executed by grassroots organizations. The July consultation will be the third one held in 2025 and will reportedly focus on projects for the Venezuelan youth.","score":0.6445166,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://freedomhouse.org/country/venezuela/freedom-world/2026","title":"Venezuela: Freedom in the World 2026 Country Report | Freedom House","content":"A country or territory’s Freedom in the World status depends on its aggregate Political Rights score, on a scale of 0–40, and its aggregate Civil Liberties score, on a scale of 0–60. Venezuela’s democratic institutions have been deteriorating since 1999, but conditions have grown sharply worse in recent years due to harsher government crackdowns on the opposition and the ruling party’s use of flawed elections to seize full control of state institutions. * A crackdown on dissidents continued throughout the year, with real and perceived opponents of President Nicolás Maduro’s government subjected to arbitrary detentions, extrajudicial killings, and forced disappearances. Independent analysts suggested that the purpose of the deployment was to exert pressure on the Maduro regime, or to prepare for military action in Venezuela.
